varieties.name: Tahitian Lime (Persian Lime)
varieties.origin: Unknown origin (likely Indo-Malayan region or Persia), thought to be hybrid of Key lime and citron or lemon, introduced to California from Tahiti in late 1800s
varieties.traits: Medium to large oval fruits (5-7cm), smooth thin green skin, nearly seedless, vigorous tree growth to 4-6m
varieties.yield_notes: High yielding, produces year-round with peak in winter-spring

varieties.name: Bearss Lime
varieties.origin: California nursery of J.T. Bearss, 1895, a selection of Tahitian lime
varieties.traits: Seedless, dark green oval fruit, smooth skin, vigorous growth
varieties.yield_notes: Very productive, main crop winter through late spring
varieties.growing_notes: California-grown variation of Tahitian lime, well-suited to Brisbane subtropical climate. Long shelf life makes it commercially valuable. Thrives in zones 9-11. Good humidity tolerance. Related to Persian lime but distinct selection.
varieties.availability: Available from specialized citrus nurseries in Queensland

varieties.name: Dwarf Tahitian Lime
varieties.origin: Dwarf selection of Tahitian lime, modern development for container growing
varieties.traits: Compact growth 1.5-2m, same fruit as standard Tahitian, ideal for pots
varieties.yield_notes: Good production for size, year-round fruiting
varieties.growing_notes: Perfect for Brisbane balconies and small gardens. Subtropical adapted, thrives in containers with good drainage. Popular at Ross Evans Garden Centre and similar Queensland retailers. Same climate tolerance as standard Tahitian.
varieties.availability: Widely available at Ross Evans Garden Centre and Queensland nurseries

varieties.name: West Indian Lime (Key Lime, Mexican Lime) 🏆
varieties.origin: Southeast Asia (Indo-Malayan region), spread via Arab traders to North Africa and Mediterranean (10th century), brought to Americas by Columbus (1493)
varieties.traits: Small round fruit (3-5cm), thin smooth yellowish-green skin, seedy, thorny shrub-like tree 2-3m

varieties.name: Sublime Lime
varieties.origin: Australia, cutting-grown selection of West Indian lime
varieties.traits: Similar to West Indian lime, non-grafted cutting-grown form
varieties.growing_notes: Australian selection for subtropical SE Queensland. Better suited if kept in pots. Cutting-grown so comes true to type. Requires warm microclimate in Brisbane.

varieties.name: Finger Lime 'Rainforest Pearl' 🌏
varieties.origin: Native Australian species from rainforests of SE Queensland and NE NSW (Brisbane to Clarence River), Bangalow NSW selection, pink-fruited form
varieties.traits: Elongated finger-shaped fruit 5-12cm, pink to pale green skin, thorny understorey shrub/small tree 2-6m, vigorous growth
varieties.yield_notes: Good production, winter through spring harvest

varieties.name: Finger Lime 'Red Centre' (Australian Red Center) 🌏
varieties.origin: Native Australian species, SE Queensland and NE NSW rainforests, selected for red coloring
varieties.traits: Finger-shaped fruit 5-10cm, reddish-brown to burgundy skin, thorny compact growth 2-5m

varieties.name: Finger Lime 'Alstonville' 🌏
varieties.origin: Native Australian, registered cultivar from NSW north coast region (close to Brisbane)
varieties.traits: Dark green-black fruit skin, tall growing shrub, registered with Australian Cultivar Registration Authority
varieties.growing_notes: NATIVE to regions near Brisbane, excellent subtropical adaptation. Registered cultivar with commercial production. Well-suited to Brisbane climate with high humidity tolerance. Indigenous Australian species.
varieties.availability: Queensland nurseries, Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery

varieties.name: Finger Lime 'Byron Sunrise' 🌏
varieties.origin: Native Australian, Byron Bay region NSW (adjacent to Queensland border), registered cultivar
varieties.traits: Grafted selection, registered with Australian Cultivar Registration Authority
varieties.growing_notes: NATIVE Australian from region directly adjacent to Brisbane, perfectly climate-matched. Indigenous species with commercial cultivation. Excellent for subtropical Queensland gardens.
varieties.availability: Grafted plants from Queensland specialist nurseries

varieties.name: Finger Lime 'Judy's Everbearing' 🌏
varieties.origin: Native Australian, registered cultivar, SE Queensland/NSW heritage
varieties.traits: Reddish to green and dark green peel, medium tree size, registered cultivar
varieties.yield_notes: Excellent production, extended bearing season (everbearing trait)
varieties.growing_notes: NATIVE Australian species, naturally adapted to Brisbane subtropical conditions. Everbearing trait provides extended harvest. Indigenous to SE Queensland region.
varieties.availability: Queensland citrus nurseries, Sustainable Gardening Australia retailers

varieties.name: Finger Lime 'Durham's Emerald' 🌏
varieties.origin: Native Australian, registered cultivar from Queensland/NSW region
varieties.traits: Green to dark green peel, registered with Australian Cultivar Registration Authority
varieties.growing_notes: NATIVE Australian, indigenous to Brisbane climate zone. Registered commercial cultivar. Well-adapted to subtropical humidity and heat.
varieties.availability: Queensland nurseries specializing in native citrus

varieties.name: Finger Lime 'Pink Ice' 🌏
varieties.origin: Native Australian, registered cultivar, Queensland/NSW rainforests
varieties.traits: Registered with Australian Cultivar Registration Authority, distinctive pink coloring

varieties.name: Finger Lime 'Jali Red' 🌏
varieties.origin: Native Australian, registered cultivar
varieties.traits: Red coloring, registered with Australian Cultivar Registration Authority
varieties.growing_notes: NATIVE Australian, suited to Brisbane subtropical climate. Indigenous species.

varieties.name: Finger Lime 'Blunobia Pink Crystal' 🌏
varieties.origin: Native Australian, registered cultivar
varieties.traits: Pink crystal-like pearls, registered cultivar
varieties.growing_notes: NATIVE Australian adapted to Brisbane subtropical conditions. Indigenous species.

varieties.name: Finger Lime 'Tasty Green' 🌏
varieties.origin: Native Australian, Queensland/NSW selection
varieties.traits: Green fruit, reputed to be best tasting finger lime variety
varieties.growing_notes: NATIVE Australian, indigenous to Brisbane region. Highly regarded for taste. Subtropical adapted.

varieties.name: Finger Lime 'Rick's Red' 🌏
varieties.origin: Native Australian, Queensland selection
varieties.traits: Red-fruited form, popular garden variety

varieties.name: Finger Lime 'Yellow' (Sunshine Yellow) 🌏
varieties.origin: Native Australian, Queensland/NSW selection
varieties.traits: Yellow-fruited form, compact growth
varieties.growing_notes: NATIVE Australian adapted to Brisbane climate. Indigenous species with good subtropical performance.

varieties.name: Finger Lime 'Collette' 🌏
varieties.origin: Native Australian, Queensland selection
varieties.traits: Popular grafted variety, flowers anytime for year-round fruit
varieties.yield_notes: Continuous production, fruit at different stages

varieties.name: Australian Blood Lime (Australian Red Centre) 🌏
varieties.origin: CSIRO Australia breeding program, 2004, cross of red finger lime and acid mandarin or Rangpur lime
varieties.traits: Small egg-shaped fruit, blood-red rind, flesh and juice, Plant Breeders Rights protected
varieties.growing_notes: Bred specifically for Australian conditions by CSIRO. Uses native Australian finger lime genetics. Well-adapted to subtropical Queensland. Licensed for commercial growing. Unique Australian development perfect for Brisbane climate.
varieties.availability: Licensed Queensland nurseries - Citrees Nursery, major garden centers

varieties.name: Australian Sunrise Lime 🌏
varieties.origin: CSIRO Australia breeding program, 2004, seedling selection of Faustrimedin hybrid
varieties.traits: Pear-shaped fruit, yellow rind, orange inside, Plant Breeders Rights protected

varieties.name: Australian Outback Lime (Australian Desert) 🌏
varieties.origin: CSIRO Australia, 2004, selected from collection of native desert lime trees (Citrus glauca)
varieties.traits: Small shrub/tree 2-12m, from arid inland Australia, extremely drought and salt tolerant
varieties.growing_notes: NATIVE Australian species adapted to extreme conditions. Drought tolerant for Brisbane dry periods. Salt tolerant for coastal areas. Desert lime genetics make it very hardy for Queensland. Can withstand high temperatures (up to 45°C) and tolerates cold to -24°C when dormant.

varieties.name: Round Lime (Dooja) - Citrus australis 🌏
varieties.origin: Native Australian species, south-eastern Australia including Queensland
varieties.traits: Round green fruits, native to SE Australia
varieties.growing_notes: NATIVE to SE Australia including Queensland regions. Indigenous species naturally adapted to Brisbane climate. Traditional Indigenous Australian food.

varieties.name: Kaffir Lime (Makrut Lime, Thai Lime)
varieties.origin: Native to tropical Southeast Asia (Indonesia, Thailand, Laos, Philippines), ancient species
varieties.traits: Distinctive bumpy warty fruit 4-6cm, thorny shrub 2-11m, unique hourglass double-lobed leaves, very aromatic
varieties.yield_notes: Good production of fruit and leaves
varieties.growing_notes: Thrives in Brisbane subtropical climate but needs warmth. Leaves are primary culinary use (Thai, Indonesian, Cambodian cuisine). Tolerates temperatures 21-35°C, sensitive to cold below 4°C. Needs protection from frost. High humidity lover - perfect for Brisbane. Grows well at Ross Evans Garden Centre and throughout Queensland. USDA zones 10-12 but manageable in zone 9 with protection.
varieties.availability: Widely available at Ross Evans Garden Centre, Queensland nurseries

varieties.name: Rangpur Lime (Mandarin Lime)
varieties.origin: India (Rangpur region), hybrid of citron and mandarin orange
varieties.traits: Medium tree 20 feet, orange-red skin resembling mandarin, called Canton lemon in China, Cravo in Brazil
varieties.growing_notes: More cold tolerant than most limes - tolerates freezes in zone 8a (10-15°F). Good for Brisbane with excellent subtropical performance. Popular for marmalade. Can be grown in ground or containers in SE Queensland.
varieties.availability: Listed in Brisbane Edible Gardening plant list, specialty nurseries

varieties.name: Limequat (Eustis Limequat)
varieties.origin: Developed by Dr. Walter Swingle, 1909, USA, cross between Key lime and kumquat
varieties.traits: Small fruit, more cold-tolerant than Key lime, green turning lemon-yellow when ripe
varieties.growing_notes: Better cold tolerance than traditional limes makes it suitable for Brisbane with some protection. Grows in temperate to subtropical zones. Good for containers. Can replace Key lime in recipes.

varieties.name: Kagzi Lime (Kargzi Lime) 🏆
varieties.origin: Traditional Indian variety, cultivated for centuries throughout India, comes true from seed
varieties.traits: Small round fruit with thin skin, spreading tree with dense small leaves, aromatic, highly polyembryonic
varieties.yield_notes: Good production, 25-30 tons per hectare, bearing from 2.5-3 years

varieties.name: Vikram
varieties.origin: Modern high-yielding selection developed in India for tropical conditions
varieties.traits: Medium fruit weight 22.8g, smooth to semi-smooth surface texture
varieties.yield_notes: High yielding, 150-500 quintals per hectare depending on conditions

varieties.name: Prumalini (Pramalini)
varieties.origin: Modern Indian selection for tropical southern regions
varieties.traits: Fruit weight 21.1g (minimum among varieties), rough surface texture
varieties.yield_notes: Very high yielding variety, 150-500 quintals per hectare

varieties.name: PKM (PKM-1)
varieties.origin: Developed by Tamil Nadu Agricultural University (TNAU), India, for southern tropical regions
varieties.traits: Well-adapted to tropical conditions, robust growth
varieties.yield_notes: High yielding commercial variety, 150-500 quintals per hectare

varieties.name: Sai Sharbati (Rashraj, Sharbati)
varieties.origin: Indian selection for tropical and subtropical regions
varieties.traits: Fruit weight 22.3g, rough surface, semi-smooth texture

varieties.name: Phule Sharbati (Pule Sharbatti)
varieties.origin: Developed in Maharashtra, India, for tropical climate zones
varieties.traits: Fruit weight 24.3g (highest among varieties), robust tree

varieties.name: Balaji
varieties.origin: Developed in Andhra Pradesh, India, for southern tropical regions
varieties.traits: Fruit weight 21.7g, adapted to Andhra conditions
varieties.yield_notes: VERY HIGH yielding variety, notable for exceptional production in Andhra Pradesh

varieties.name: PDKV Variety
varieties.origin: Developed in Maharashtra, India, for Maharashtra's tropical climate
varieties.traits: Regional Maharashtra selection

varieties.name: Puliyangudi Acid Lime 🌏 🏆 (GI Application Pending)
varieties.origin: Puliyangudi region, Tamil Nadu (near Kerala border, 140km SW of Madurai), foothills of Western Ghats
varieties.traits: Paper-thin 2mm peel, 50-55% juice content, 8% citric acid (vs 5% in other varieties), highly aromatic
varieties.yield_notes: ~10,000 tonnes annually from 7,500 hectares, 1000+ fruits per tree (up from 700-750 a decade ago)

varieties.name: West Indian Lime (Key Lime, Mexican Lime) 🏆 🌱
varieties.origin: Southeast Asia (Indo-Malayan region - INDIA included), ancient variety spread via Arab traders, centuries of cultivation in India
varieties.traits: Small round fruit 3-5cm, thin smooth yellowish-green skin, very seedy, thorny shrubby tree 2-3m, highly polyembryonic
varieties.yield_notes: Good production in tropical conditions, year-round bearing

varieties.name: Tahitian Lime (Persian Lime)
varieties.origin: Thought to originate from NW India/Persia, hybrid of Key lime and citron or lemon
varieties.traits: Medium to large oval fruits 5-7cm, smooth thin green skin, nearly seedless, vigorous growth 4-6m
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT for Kerala tropical monsoon climate. May have originated in NW India making it inherently suited to Indian conditions. Thrives in hot humid tropical regions. More heat and humidity tolerant than generally credited. Good disease resistance. Widely cultivated globally and can adapt well to Thiruvalla conditions with adequate water during monsoon.

varieties.name: Kaffir Lime (Makrut Lime, Thai Lime) 🌱
varieties.origin: NATIVE to tropical Southeast Asia including INDIA - specifically recorded in Sri Lanka and Indian usage since 1800s, ancient species
varieties.traits: Distinctive bumpy warty fruit 4-6cm, thorny shrub 2-11m, unique hourglass double-lobed leaves, intensely aromatic
varieties.yield_notes: Good leaf and fruit production year-round in tropics
varieties.growing_notes: PERFECT for Kerala tropical monsoon climate - this is its NATIVE HABITAT. Thrives in warm humid tropical conditions 21-35°C. INDIGENOUS to Indian subcontinent (Sri Lanka historical records). Loves high humidity - IDEAL for Thiruvalla monsoon. Recorded in India since 1888, called 'Kudalu dchi' (Leech Lime) in Sri Lanka/India. Grows as ornamental and culinary plant throughout Kerala. Leaves essential for South Indian cuisine. USDA zones 10-12 - Kerala is perfect.
varieties.availability: Widely available Kerala nurseries, traditional plantings, Thiruvalla agricultural centers

varieties.name: Sweet Lime (Mosambi, Mausambi, Palestine Sweet Lime) 🏆 🌱
varieties.origin: Native to Central and Northern India (Meghalaya, Nagaland, Tamil Nadu, Punjab hills), ancient cultivation, brought to South Asia from Mozambique by Portuguese, cultivated for centuries
varieties.traits: Small round 5-8cm, pale green to orange-yellow when ripe, thin to thick peel varieties, medium tree 7-8m, thorny, irregular spreading growth
varieties.yield_notes: Good production, begins bearing 5-7 years, peak 10-20 years

varieties.name: Rangpur Lime (Mandarin Lime) 🧬
varieties.origin: INDIA - Rangpur region, hybrid of citron and mandarin orange, indigenous to Indian subcontinent
varieties.traits: Medium tree, orange-red skin resembling mandarin, called different names across Asia (Canton lemon in China, Cravo in Brazil, hime lemon in Japan)

varieties.name: Assam Lemon 🏆 🌱
varieties.origin: Native to Assam region, NE India, traditional Indian variety
varieties.traits: Traditional lemon variety from northeastern India
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate to good in tropical conditions

varieties.name: Italian Lemon
varieties.origin: Mediterranean origin, introduced to India
varieties.traits: Mediterranean variety adapted to Indian conditions

varieties.name: Pant Lemon 🌱
varieties.origin: Developed in India, modern Indian selection
varieties.traits: Indian-developed variety

varieties.name: Galgal (Hill Lemon) 🏆 🌱
varieties.origin: Native to hills of India (Himalayan foothills), traditional Indian citrus, ancient cultivation
varieties.traits: Large rough fruit, traditional hill variety from Indian Himalayas

varieties.name: Eureka Lemon
varieties.origin: California, USA, 1858, Mediterranean ancestry
varieties.traits: Medium tree, four-season bearer, true lemon

varieties.name: Seedless Lime
varieties.origin: Modern selection developed for Indian conditions
varieties.traits: Seedless or very few seeds, modern convenience variety

varieties.name: Jai Devi 🌱
varieties.origin: Indian selection for tropical regions
varieties.traits: Regional Indian variety
varieties.yield_notes: Good in tropical hot and humid climates

varieties.name: Rasraj
varieties.origin: Indian selection, another name for Rashraj/Sharbati type
varieties.traits: Similar to Sai Sharbati

varieties.name: Sevilla Lemon (Seville Orange/Lemon) 🏆
varieties.origin: Seville, Spain, Mediterranean origin, brought to India, ancient variety from Moorish Spain
varieties.traits: Mediterranean variety popular in South India, traditional
